steps.title: Find the proxy assumption
steps.content: The hypothesis is that CuO-NP bioaccumulation in L. stagnalis could stand in for costly sampling of X. laevis. That requires the snail data to predict the frog data reliably; if uptake differs in an unclear way, the comparison breaks down for compliance testing.

steps.title: Undermine the comparison
steps.content: Choice A shows consistency, which helps the hypothesis. Choice C concerns other nanoparticles, and Choice D gives a practical advantage of using snails. A poorly understood difference in uptake rates between the two species would make the proxy unreliable. The answer is B.
